message:
Does your cat ever walk up, gently bonk their forehead against you, and rub their face along your cheek or hand? 🐱 That sweet little headbutt has a name — "bunting" — and it's one of the biggest compliments a cat can give.

Cats have scent glands on their cheeks, chin, and forehead, and when they press and rub against you, they're depositing their own personal pheromones. To a cat, that means they're marking you as safe, familiar, and part of the family — literally claiming you as theirs in the gentlest way possible. In the wild, cats who live together blend their scents this way to create one shared "we belong together" smell. Many experts think bunting even releases feel-good endorphins, so it's soothing for them too.

So the next time your cat head-butts you, take it as the love note it really is. You've officially been chosen. 💛

Is your cat a headbutter? Tell me where they like to bonk you most! 😻🐾
